title: Implement Remix 3 example
description: Add examples/remix-3/ per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-22-remix-3-example-design.md following the plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-22-remix-3-example.md. 14-task implementation, subagent-driven.
close_reason: Dev server works end-to-end (home list, post detail via Frame soft-nav, CopyCode clientEntry island hydrates and copies). Typecheck green. Build/preview blocked by upstream Remix 3 plugin issue tracked separately in bd.

title: Add Waku RSC example
description: Create a new example under examples/waku that mirrors the react-router-rsc, tanstack-start-rsc, and redwood-sdk examples using Waku primitives for routing and RSC. Use Arktype as the Standard Schema implementation instead of data-schema/Zod/Valibot.
close_reason: Added examples/waku with Arktype; fixed content-layer plugin + ResolveSchema to support callable Standard Schema types

title: Add RedwoodSDK RSC example
description: Create a new example under examples/redwood-sdk that mirrors the react-router-rsc and tanstack-start-rsc examples, but uses RedwoodSDK's primitives (rwsdk/worker, rwsdk/router, rwsdk/client) and Cloudflare Workers runtime via @cloudflare/vite-plugin. Uses Zod (instead of data-schema or Valibot) for the content schema. Demonstrates: glob loader for MDX blog posts, file loader for JSON authors, reference() resolution, rendering MDX inside an async server route component, and a 'use client' CopyCode island.
close_reason: Added examples/redwood-sdk — RedwoodSDK + Cloudflare Workers example mirroring react-router-rsc and tanstack-start-rsc, using Zod for the content schema. Home listing, blog post route, MDX rendering via Content Layer, and a 'use client' CopyCode island all verified end-to-end in dev server.

title: Make reference() assignable in Valibot/Zod v.object() / z.object()
description: Content-layer's README advertises compat with Zod, Valibot, ArkType, and others, but reference() returns a ReferenceSchema whose shape only satisfies @remix-run/data-schema's Schema type (Standard Schema + a '~run(value, context) => Result' method). Valibot's v.object() requires every child to satisfy its BaseSchema interface — kind/type/reference/expects/async fields plus a '~run(dataset, config) => OutputDataset' method — and Zod v4's z.object() likely has similar requirements. Passing reference('authors') into v.object() today fails with TS2322: missing properties kind, type, reference, expects, async. The two '~run' signatures are structurally incompatible; a dual-shape '~run' implementation would need runtime arg-sniffing and an intersection type. Scope: decide whether to make reference() extend Valibot's BaseSchema at the cost of data-schema type compat (runtime still fine via '~standard.validate'), or provide per-library helpers (valibotReference/zodReference), or adopt a runtime-dispatch dual '~run'. Discovered from the tanstack-start-rsc example switching to Valibot.
design: Options: (A) Change reference()'s ~run to Valibot's (dataset, config) shape and break data-schema; fix RR example. (B) Intersect ~run as an overloaded function that supports both signatures, with runtime dispatch based on arg shape. (C) Ship library-specific helpers alongside reference().
acceptance_criteria: reference('authors') is accepted as a field value inside Valibot's v.object({...}) at the type level. Schema inference through getCollection/getEntry still produces {collection, id} output. The React Router data-schema example still typechecks (or has a documented migration path).

title: Add TanStack Start RSC example
description: Create examples/tanstack-start-rsc/ that mirrors the react-router-rsc example using TanStack Start's experimental RSC primitives (renderServerComponent, createCompositeComponent, createServerFn, createFileRoute). Should use the same content.config.ts semantics (glob for MDX posts, file for authors.json, reference() from post to author) and showcase a use client component inside MDX.
acceptance_criteria: Home page lists sorted blog posts with links. Post page shows title, author (resolved via reference), date, and MDX body. CopyCode 'use client' component works inside MDX. Uses @tanstack/react-start RSC helpers. Routes use createFileRoute. Content-layer config + collection discovery are unchanged.
close_reason: TanStack Start RSC example added: build passes, preview serves 200 for home+post routes, RSC stream correctly includes resolved author data, MDX content, and a client reference for CopyCode. Typecheck + lint + fmt clean.

title: Add Husky pre-commit hook for lockfile drift + staged lint/format
description:
The v0.1.4 release was blocked when @tailwindcss/typography drift between examples/react-router-rsc/package.json and pnpm-lock.yaml slipped past review. CI caught it via vp install --frozen-lockfile, but at that point the git tag and GitHub release already existed and had to be deleted and recreated.

Add a Husky-managed pre-commit hook that runs:
1. vp install --frozen-lockfile — fails the commit if the lockfile is out of sync
2. vp staged — runs oxfmt and oxlint on staged files only

Install Husky as a root devDep, add prepare script, write .husky/pre-commit. Verify the hook blocks a staged lockfile-drift scenario and passes on a clean staged change.
acceptance_criteria:
- .husky/pre-commit exists, tracked in git, and is executable
- Root package.json has a prepare script that runs husky
- husky is a devDep
- Hook fails the commit when lockfile drifts from package.json
- Hook passes on a clean commit
- Hook runs staged-file formatting and linting on commit
close_reason: Added vp's native commit-hook integration. Appended two checks below the beads-managed section in .beads/hooks/pre-commit: (1) vp install --frozen-lockfile, gated on package.json or pnpm-lock.yaml being staged; (2) vp staged using a new staged block in vite.config.ts that runs vp fmt on text files and vp lint on JS/TS. Verified both paths: hook exits 1 and prints a clear message when drift is staged, exits 0 and runs fmt/lint on clean commits.

title: Add type-level tests for schema propagation through the content-layer type chain
description:
The public API exposes schema-derived types through a chain: Standard Schema → InferSchemaOutput → DataEntry<...> → CollectionEntry<C> → getCollection/getEntry/getEntries/render/reference. Today the chain is untested at the type level; regressions like the ones fixed in v0.1.2 and v0.1.3 (types widening to any, reference collection string widening) surfaced only in downstream projects.

Add Vitest typecheck mode + .test-d.ts files covering:
- InferSchemaOutput pulls ~standard.types.output correctly
- ResolveSchema unwraps function-form schemas
- reference<C>() preserves the collection literal
- The generated virtual module's getCollection/getEntry/getEntries/render propagate the schema output into CollectionEntry.data
- CollectionEntry.collection is a string literal, not widened to string

Also add a drift-guard test in codegen.test.ts that asserts generateTypes() output still references the exported helper types, so the hand-synced declare module fixture in codegen.test-d.ts can't silently rot.
acceptance_criteria:
- vp test runs .test-d.ts files and reports type errors as test failures
- types.test-d.ts covers InferSchemaOutput, ResolveSchema, and reference<C>() literal preservation
- codegen.test-d.ts covers the full getCollection/getEntry/getEntries/render/reference chain through a simulated declare module block
- codegen.test.ts contains a drift-guard asserting generateTypes() output references InferSchemaOutput and ResolveSchema from @withsprinkles/content-layer
- InferSchemaOutput and ResolveSchema are exported from the package root
close_reason: Added Vitest typecheck (checker=tsgo) wired via packages/content-layer/vite.config.ts. Extracted InferSchemaOutput and ResolveSchema from inlined codegen into types.ts exports; codegen now references them via @withsprinkles/content-layer. types.test-d.ts covers the helpers + reference<C>() literal preservation. codegen.test-d.ts mirrors the generator's declare-module shape and asserts schema output propagates through getCollection/getEntry/getEntries/render/reference, plus .collection stays a string literal. Drift-guard test in codegen.test.ts asserts the generator output still references the two helpers from the package.

title: findCollectionForEntry scans by ID and can resolve to the wrong collection
description:
In packages/content-layer/src/runtime.ts:50-57, `findCollectionForEntry(entry)` walks every store and returns the first one whose store contains `entry.id`. That ID-only lookup ignores the collection the entry actually belongs to — which is information we already have.

When two collections happen to have an entry with the same `id`, the function returns the wrong collection name. `findImporter` (runtime.ts:59-69) then builds the key `${collectionName}/${entry.id}`, which selects the wrong MDX importer and silently renders the wrong content — or throws `No content found for entry "..."` if the other collection doesn't have a matching importer slot.

Not exercised by the current example (`examples/react-router-rsc/` has slug-based blog IDs like `hello-world` and short author IDs like `jane`, which don't collide), but the fragility will bite any user whose collections share any ID.

Discovered during the Task 5 code review of the React Router RSC example (2026-04-22). Code reviewer's summary: "findCollectionForEntry uses ID matching, not collection-scoped lookup."
acceptance_criteria:
- `findCollectionForEntry` either uses the entry's `collection` field or is removed in favor of threading `collection` through the call chain
- A regression test exists that fails without the fix: two collections with the same entry ID, assert `render()` reaches the correct importer
- No behavior change for consumers whose IDs already don't collide
close_reason: Fixed: replaced findCollectionForEntry ID-scan with entry.collection lookup. Added regression test covering two collections sharing an ID.

title: Upstream: Remix 3 template's vp build fails with undefined environment
description:
The Remix 3 example's production build fails because remix.plugin.ts assumes builder.environments.ssr exists at buildApp time, but under current Vite+ (0.1.16 and 0.1.19) the SSR environment isn't instantiated. The same error reproduces in ~/Developer/Templates/remix-3-templates/default — this is not workspace-specific.

Error: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'isBuilt')
  at builder.build (...remix.plugin.ts:24:23)
  at buildApp (...remix.plugin.ts:42:19)

pnpm dev works fine. examples/remix-3 lands with a working dev server; build/preview are blocked until upstream fixes Remix 3's fullstack plugin integration with Vite+. Do NOT patch remix.plugin.ts locally — keep it as a verbatim copy of the template's plugin.
close_reason: Fixed by adding builder: {} to examples/remix-3/vite.config.ts (commit c41c889). Root cause: remix plugin's config() hook doesn't set config.builder, so Vite falls back to legacy single-environment mode and builder.environments.ssr comes back undefined. Adding a small plugin that returns { builder: {} } enables multi-env build without patching remix.plugin.ts itself. Apps using @cloudflare/vite-plugin worked around this implicitly because cloudflare sets config.builder.

value: Remix 3's css() mixins emit their rules inside @layer rmx. Any unlayered CSS (preflight resets, element-level styles) will BEAT every layered rule due to cascade-layer semantics — unlayered > any layer, regardless of declaration order. So if you ship a preflight like 'h1 { font-size: inherit }' unlayered, your css() mixin rules like .rmxc-xxx { font-size: 1.5rem } will silently lose. Fix: wrap the preflight in @layer base { ... } and declare the order at the top of the CSS entry: '@layer base, rmx;'. Now base < rmx and your mixins win. Keep :root custom-property declarations UNLAYERED because custom properties cascade by inheritance, not layer order. Same consideration applies to any future Remix 3 + Vite+ setup with a Tailwind-preflight-style reset.

In Remix 3, run() from 'remix/component' installs its own Frame-aware navigation interceptor. An entry.browser.ts only needs run({loadModule, resolveFrame}) — nothing more — to get working soft nav via Frames. The default template's custom navigation.addEventListener('navigate', ...) handler exists only to layer on form submission interception and manual focus/scroll management. For content sites / read-only apps with no forms, drop that listener entirely.

Remix 3 fetch-router: get('/')/post('/')/etc return a single Route, not a RouteMap. Controllers with { actions: {...} } satisfy Controller<RouteMap>, which expects multiple named actions (like form() produces with its index/action pair). For single-method routes with one handler per file, export a bare async function and satisfy Action<method, pattern>: export default (async ctx => { ... }) satisfies Action<'GET', '/blog/:slug'>. Wire with router.map(routes.x, handler) — map() accepts a Route and threads the pattern's params into ctx.params. Don't use router.get('/', handler) in apps that declare routes in a separate module, since that duplicates the URL string.

Remix 3: renderToStream() on the server needs an explicit resolveFrame callback when rendering a component tree containing <Frame>. Without it, defaultResolveFrame throws 'No resolveFrame provided'. The client's run({resolveFrame}) only handles client-side soft-nav refetches — it does NOT cover the initial SSR where Frames resolve their src within the same process. Implementation: import the router from entry.server.tsx into your render helper, then pass resolveFrame: async (src, target, ctx) => router.fetch(new Request(url, { headers: { 'x-remix-frame': target, accept: 'text/html' } })).body. Also pass frameSrc: context.url so the first frame knows what the current URL is. Mirror the default template's app/utils/render.tsx.

Plans executed by subagents MUST NOT create intermediate commits that fail the workspace's pre-commit hook. In this repo the hook runs 'vp fmt' + 'vp lint' (type-aware), so every commit must typecheck green. If a task can't produce green types on its own (e.g. it adds a file whose import target is created in a later task), combine those tasks into one commit rather than relying on subagents not to --no-verify. Haiku implementers will rationalize around hook failures by adding --no-verify despite explicit instructions otherwise — the plan structure, not the prompt wording, is the durable fix.

Remix 3 default template's pnpm build fails out of the box because its remix.plugin.ts config() hook sets config.environments but not config.builder. Vite's createBuilder falls back to legacy single-environment mode when config.builder is undefined, so builder.environments.ssr comes back undefined at buildApp time and throws 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading isBuilt)'. Fix: add a tiny plugin to vite.config.ts that returns { builder: {} } from its config() hook — this flips Vite into multi-environment mode. Apps using @cloudflare/vite-plugin work around this implicitly because cloudflare sets config.builder. For non-cloudflare / Node-targeted apps, the builder: {} shim is the minimal fix. Do not patch remix.plugin.ts itself — the fix belongs in the user's vite.config.ts so the plugin stays byte-identical to the upstream template.

examples/redwood-sdk requires three Vite+ × rwsdk workarounds not needed in vanilla Vite/rwsdk: (1) environments.worker/ssr.optimizeDeps.exclude for rwsdk/__ssr and rwsdk/__ssr_bridge — condition-gated entries can't be co-bundled; (2) src/client.tsx must import src/stub-webpack-require.ts FIRST (stubs globalThis.__webpack_require__ to a noop) because Rolldown hoists react-server-dom-webpack's import above rwsdk's setWebpackRequire body in the merged chunk; (3) src/client.tsx must ALSO import 'virtual:vite-preamble' manually — rwsdk's transformJsxScriptTagsPlugin scans for jsx('script'/link, ...) literal patterns in worker env .tsx files to detect client entry points, and Vite+'s JSX output doesn't match. Also: Document component must pull nonce from DocumentProps and apply it to the inline <script>import('/src/client.tsx')</script> or the setCommonHeaders CSP blocks hydration. MDX files that import client islands must use the #/* package.json imports field, not the tsconfig @/* alias — path aliases don't resolve from the virtual:sprinkles-content/entry/* module id.
